content: I'm looking to upgrade my photography setup and need advice on cameras. I used to shoot with a Nikon D750 but I'm considering switching systems entirely since I've been getting into videography more seriously.

That's an interesting transition! What kind of videography are you focusing on? The camera choice really depends on whether you're doing run-and-gun documentary style or more controlled restaurant and food videography.

Mostly restaurant and food content for my social media and website. I own a small restaurant in Portland and I've been documenting the farm-to-table process, including my beekeeping and mushroom foraging trips.

That sounds fascinating! For food videography, you might want something with excellent color science and in-body stabilization. Have you considered mirrorless options like the Sony A7S III or Panasonic S5?

I've heard good things about the Sony, but I'm also looking at the Canon EOS R6. I switched careers from journalism to being a chef about 3 years ago, and photography has been a big part of building my restaurant's brand.

The Canon R6 would be excellent for your needs - great low-light performance for indoor restaurant shots and fantastic color for food. Plus the dual card slots are useful for important shoots. What lenses do you currently have?

I have a 24-70mm f/2.8 and a 50mm prime. I'm thinking about adding a macro lens for the mushroom photography - I find the textures and details fascinating. My garden is where I grow most of the produce for the restaurant.

The macro lens is a great idea for capturing the intricate details of mushrooms and other ingredients. I also do a lot of salsa dancing on weekends, so I need something relatively compact to carry to events.

For mushroom photography, a 90-100mm macro would be perfect - enough working distance to not disturb the subjects. The R6 with those lenses would be a versatile setup. Have you thought about lighting equipment for indoor food shots?

I've been using natural light mostly, but I know I need to upgrade. I'm also curious about editing software - I currently use Lightroom but I'm wondering if I should switch to something more video-focused.

For your mixed photo and video workflow, Adobe Premiere Pro with Lightroom integration would be ideal. The R6 paired with your current lenses and a good LED panel would give you everything you need for stunning restaurant and foraging content.
